lesson.menu_desc: Dialogue + describing a simple report with There is / There are (chart, columns, rows, total) -- 30 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 11 -- The Report -- Presenting a Simple Report to David
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Maísa de Oliveira Santos | Aula 11 -- The Report

listenings.text: Hi Maisa, this is David. Let me describe my report to you. On the first page, there is one big chart. There are three columns in the chart: one for January, one for February, and one for March. There are also five rows, one for each client. At the bottom of the table, there is a total. On page two, there is one more figure and a small note. All the data is correct. Please check it and tell me what you think. Thank you!

Hello, this is Sarah from the finance team. I want to tell you about the report on my screen. There is a table with a lot of data. There are four columns: the name, the date, the price, and the total. There are six rows in the table. On the right side, there is a small chart with three bars. There is one total at the bottom of the page. If there is a figure you do not understand, please ask me. I am happy to explain the data. Have a great day!

extra_audio.text: Good morning, David. Here is my report. There is one main chart on the first page. There are three columns for the numbers. Is there a total at the bottom? Thank you very much.
